name: North Savo (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the sable field of the Savo arms
the golden fly tips and the golden charges of the arms

description: A black swallow-tailed flag with golden fly tips, charged centrally with the elaborate provincial arms of North Savo — a crowned arm drawing a bow, with Savonian frontier motifs.
history: North Savo (Pohjois-Savo) became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic arms of the Savo province.

features.symbolism: a heraldic composition derived from the historic provincial arms of Savo, depicting a crowned arm drawing a bow with additional Savonian frontier motifs in gold, silver and red.
